answer:
Alright, so let me think for a second. I guess the most memorable time I made a connection with someone was back in college. There was this guy named Alex who sat next to me in my psychology class. At first, we just exchanged a few words about the lectures, but one day, I noticed he had a sticker of my favorite band on his laptop—The Killers, if you're curious. Right away, I brought it up, and we started talking about music. Turns out, not only did we have similar taste in bands, but we also both played guitar.

So, after that, we started hanging out outside of class, jamming together and even going to some local gigs. Honestly, it felt like we'd known each other for years, even though we'd just met. It was refreshing to find someone who genuinely shared my passions, you know? That connection meant a lot to me because, at that point, I was feeling pretty homesick and kind of out of place in a new city. Meeting Alex helped me break out of my shell and make college feel more like home. Looking back, I’d say that friendship shaped my entire college experience for the better.
title:
Discuss a time when you made a connection with someone.
 Who was the person and how did you meet them? 
What did you have in common? 
Why was this connection meaningful to you?
